message:
My 10-year-old asked me last week why we have a 'boring bag' sitting in the hallway closet. I tried to explain it was our emergency go-bag, and she just looked at me like I'd said we were training for the moon.

Then I realized—I'd never actually *shown* her what was in it, or why. So we unpacked it together, and suddenly it wasn't boring anymore. She got to hold the flashlight, reorganize the first-aid stuff (very seriously), and ask about every single item. By the end, she was like, "Mom, this is kind of smart."

Turns out when kids are part of the conversation instead of just... being prepared *for*... they actually care. Plus now she remembers where it is, what's in it, and why it matters.

Do your kids know what's in your emergency kit, or is it one of those things you've been meaning to walk them through?
